data.title: FTC reverses orders barring John Hess from Chevron board, Sheffield from Exxon
data.description:
The Federal Trade Commission on Thursday set aside orders barring Hess (NYSE:HES) CEO John Hess from joining Chevron's (CVX) board and former Pioneer Natural Resources CEO Scott Sheffield from joining Exxon Mobil's (XOM) board (I, II).

The FTC has been reviewing orders issued during the Biden administration that barred Hess from Chevron's (CVX) board after announcing plans to acquire his company, and a similar order keeping Sheffield off Exxon's (XOM) board after the purchase of his former company, Pioneer Natural Resources.

The agency had claimed both executives had sought to communicate with OPEC about oil pricing and production, allegations they denied.

Exxon (XOM) bought Pioneer last year for $63B, while Chevron's (CVX) proposed $53B takeover of Hess (NYSE:HES) is still pending.

data.title: Google digital advertising trial delayed in Texas
data.description:
• A federal judge in Texas agreed to delay a jury trial that deals with Google's (NASDAQ:GOOG) digital advertising practices.
  • The trial was set to start on August 11.
  • Judge Sean Jordan granted Google's (NASDAQ:GOOGL) motion for the delay believing that final resolution of a similar case against the company in a Virginia federal court could narrow the scope of the issues in the Texas case.
  • In April, the Virginia judge ruled that the Department of Justice had demonstrated that the tech giant has held a monopoly in online advertising technology in at least two markets, adding that it dominated ad spaces by having an illegal stronghold in markets for publisher ad servers and the market for ad exchanges. Google has appealed the decision.
  • Judge Leonie Brinkema later set Sept. 22 as a trial date to discuss potential remedies from both sides. 
  • Court documents in Texas show plaintiffs, which include Texas, 16 other states, and Puerto Rico, are seeking more than $100B, Reuters reported.

data.title: Gold edges lower on stronger dollar, easing fears of Powell firing
data.description:
Gold futures turned lower Thursday, pressured by a stronger U.S. dollar and reduced concerns that President Trump may attempt to fire Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

Gold gained more than 1% in the previous session on reports that Trump was set to fire Powell soon, but the president later described such a move as "highly unlikely."

"Although markets calmed after the president's remarks, uncertainty remains over possible political interference in the Fed," MUFG analyst Soojin Kim said in a note. "Any move to oust Powell before his term ends in 2026 could undermine central bank independence and further lift demand for safe-haven assets."

The dollar gained following strong U.S. economic data, with retail sales rising 0.6% last month, rebounding more than expected, and initial jobless claims fell 7,000 to a seasonally adjusted 221,000 for the week, suggesting a modest improvement in economic activity and giving the Federal Reserve cover to delay cutting interest rates while it gauges the inflation fallout from tariffs.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for July delivery closed -0.4% to $3,340.10/oz, its third loss in the past four days, but front-month Comex July silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.5% to $38.056/oz, its fourth gain in six sessions.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Also, palladium (XPDUSD:CUR) jumped 3.8% to $1,277.78, reaching its highest level since September 2023, with analysts saying fears of an escalating war in Russia - a major palladium exporter - are causing supply concerns and driving prices higher, and platinum popped 3.1% to $1,460.13.

data.title: Trump said to broaden 401(k) access to crypto investments
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is set to sign an executive order that would unlock the U.S. retirement market for alternative investments, including crypto, gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and private equity funds, according to a media report published Thursday.

The order could come as soon as this week, the Financial Times reported, citing three people with knowledge of the plans, in a move that would broaden the 401(k) menu to include investments beyond the traditional mix of stocks, bonds and mutual funds.

If enacted, Federal agencies in Washington would be directed to identify and remove remaining regulatory barriers that prevent professionally managed 401(k) plans from offering exposure to nontraditional assets, the people added.

Trump's latest policy push underscores his broader effort to bring crypto into the financial mainstream. The U.S. House of Representative has passed the CLARITY Act, a crypto market structure bill, and is set to vote on the GENIUS Act, which will regulate stablecoins, later on Thursday.

The executive order would be a boon for private capital giants Blackstone (BX), BlackRock (BLK) and Apollo Global Management (APO), the article said, noting the groups have staked their long-term growth on managing the retirement savings of everyday retirement savers.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: GE Aerospace snaps six straight sessions of gains
data.description:
GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) shares snapped six straight sessions of gains, as the stock closed 2.22% lower at $260.28 on Thursday.

The company gained about 6.3% in the preceding six sessions. Overall, the stock has gained more than 57% so far this year, compared to the nearly 7% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

GE is up nearly 11% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.57% higher on Wednesday at $266.18.

GE’s Q2 profit jumped to $2.39B, or $1.87/share, from $1.45B, or $1.20/share, in the year-earlier quarter, and revenues rose 21.2% Y/Y to $11.02B, as profit at the commercial engines and services segment - which generates more than 70% of the company's revenue - surged 33% Y/Y to $2.23B, while revenues rose 30% to $7.99B.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, GE has a Hold rating with a score of 3.46 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability and an F in the valuation factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 16 out of 19 analysts gave GE a Buy and above. Three analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts are also cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

Seeking Alpha analyst Allka Research rated GE as a Sell, stating that GE Aerospace is trading near multi-year highs, it has missed consensus revenue estimates in three of the last four quarters, making me wonder if "investors' love" for it is justified.

“GE Aerospace may face headwinds from China's aggressive push to export its own COMAC C919 jet and the ACAE CJ-1000A engine being developed for it,” added Allka Research.

data.title: GitLab launches new platform to deepen collaboration between developers and AI agents
data.description:
GitLab (NASDAQ:GTLB) has launched a public beta version of GitLab Duo Agent Platform, a platform designed to create asynchronous collaboration between developers and artificial intelligence agents. 

Developers can utilize multiple agents in workflows to make precise changes to codebases and repositories, the company said. It also features a more sophisticated chat experience with the AI agents. The platform offers various IDEs support and Model Context Protocol client support as well.

"GitLab Duo Agent Platform isn't just another AI tool; it's a fundamental reimagining of software development from isolated, linear processes into dynamic, intelligent collaboration," said GitLab CEO Bill Staples. "By leveraging GitLab's unique position as the system of record for the entire software development lifecycle, we're providing AI agents with unprecedented context and capabilities. This enables our customers to work with AI agents that have comprehensive context about their codebase, their workflows, and their organizational goals to help boost productivity, velocity, and efficiency."

The new public beta version is available today for Premium and Ultimate customers. General availability is expected by the end of the year.

data.title: FOXO Technologies announces reverse stock split
data.description:
• FOXO Technologies (NYSE:FOXO) announced on Thursday that its board of directors has approved a 1 for 1.99 reverse stock split of the company’s class A common stock. 
  • The reverse stock split will become effective on July 27, 2025. The common stock will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis on the NYSE American under the same trading symbol “FOXO” when the market opens on July 28, 2025, and will trade under a new CUSIP number.
  • As a result of the reverse stock split, every 1.99 shares of common Stock issued and outstanding at the Effective Time will be automatically combined into one share of Common Stock, with no change in the $0.0001 par value per share. 
  • The reverse stock split is being effected to ensure compliance with NYSE American continued listing requirements to have a stock price trading above $0.10 per share. 
  • FOXO -3.93% after hours to $0.1197.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hyliion secures $1.5M contract from U.S. Navy
data.description:
• Hyliion (NYSE:HYLN) announced on Thursday it has been awarded a Phase II Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) contract of $1.5 million from the U.S. Navy to further develop and refine its scalable multi-megawatt platform for shipboard and stationary military applications.
  • Hyliion expects Phase II to commence immediately and continue for a period of 18 months.
  • HYLN +2.53% after hours to $1.62.
  • Source: Press Release
